title: Chat struct uses Option fields for kind-specific data instead of a sum type
description: The Chat struct has 10+ optional fields annotated with inline comments (direct only, group only, channel only). This means the type does not prevent a value of kind=direct from having a space_id, or a kind=group from having a contact_id. The compiler offers no help; validity is only in the comments. The problem shape is a sum type. Rewrite: represent the kind-specific data as enum ChatDetails { Direct { contact_id: Id }, Group { name: String, description: Option<String>, members: Vec<ChatMember> }, Channel { name: String, space_id: Id, ... } } and embed it in Chat as pub details: ChatDetails. Shared fields remain on the outer struct. Serde representation uses untagged or internally-tagged enum to preserve the wire format.
close_reason: Deferred: large architectural refactor; separate epic needed when Chat spec stabilizes
